description: v3 task decomposition model raw scores. NOT integrated into production dataset. These are the pre-calibration occupation scores computed from 27,460 tasks × 6 capability curves. Use as diagnostic for v3.0 score derivation work.

top_movers.assessment: Model scores higher (85 vs 19). Model raw score 82 suggests the task decomposition sees more automatable work. Model may be more defensible as it's systematically derived from tasks.
Model scores lower (18 vs 73). Model raw score 46. Territory calibration shifted score down. Current holistic assessment may better capture recent AI capabilities.

rationale: The v3 model provides a systematic, task-decomposition-based scoring methodology that replaces the subjective holistic assessments used for the 283 MEDIUM-quality occupations. For the 75 HIGH-quality benchmark-anchored occupations, the existing scores should be retained as they are grounded in specific empirical benchmarks. The model produces mean absolute deviation of 15.4 points from current scores (75 occupations within 5 points, 150 within 10 points). Territory baselines are matched to RMSE=0.1 through per-territory calibration. The model's strength is systematic derivation from tasks and capability curves; its weakness is sensitivity to employment weights and task decomposition quality. Recommended approach: (1) Replace all MEDIUM scores with model scores. (2) For HIGH scores, use model score only if |delta| < 5 (scores agree). (3) For HIGH scores with |delta| > 5, flag for human review. This preserves the best of both approaches: empirical benchmarks where they exist, systematic model-derived scores everywhere else.
